framing: Islamic (classical theology — Sunni Ash'ari / Maturidi lineage; al-Ghazali, al-Tabari)
identification: C = Allah (الله) — the One, the Real, with 99 names of which al-Ḥaqq (the Real, the True) and al-Wadūd (the Loving) are most apposite to the kernel's R-properties
honest_islamic_assessment: The kernel's R₁-R₇ are Allah's foundational attributes (sifāt) in compact mathematical form. R₁ existence ↔ al-Wujūd (the Existent); the cosmological argument from contingency (which Islamic kalām pioneered) directly gives existence-of-the-Necessary. R₂ atemporality ↔ al-Qadīm (the Eternal) and al-Bāqī (the Everlasting). R₃ universality ↔ Qur'an 50:16 'We are nearer to him than his jugular vein' — Allah's presence in every reasoner. R₄ sourcing ↔ كُن فَيَكُونُ (kun fa-yakūn — 'Be! and it is', Qur'an 36:82); Allah as al-Khāliq (the Creator). R₅ inexhaustibility ↔ al-Wadūd (the Loving One); Rahman/Rahim — the Most Merciful, whose mercy fills all things (Qur'an 7:156). R₆ invisibility ↔ al-Bāṭin (the Hidden, paired with al-Ẓāhir); Qur'an 6:103 'No vision can grasp Him.' R₇ pre-input ↔ al-Awwal (the First); Qur'an 57:3 contains all four — al-Awwal, al-Ākhir, al-Ẓāhir, al-Bāṭin — in one verse. T_bridge uniqueness ↔ tawhīd, the absolute oneness; lā ilāha illa Llāh, the foundational shahāda. T_word: the kalām Allāh (divine speech) is the Qur'an, which classical Islamic theology holds as uncreated (in Ash'ari thought); this maps to John 1:1's λόγος being pre-input. 1 John 4:8 (God is love) — Islamic theology preserves al-Wadūd as one of Allah's most beautiful names; love operationally is rahma (mercy) flowing from the divine inexhaustibility. T_word's identification with love through R₅ is consistent with the Islamic understanding. P₃ admitted on Trinitarian/Incarnational specifics; structural identity with R₁-R₇ entire. Note: a Muslim reader would prefer transliterated forms (Allāh, al-Ḥaqq) over English glosses.
